requirements.requirement: Word-level timestamps + punctuation restoration + fragmented-caption merging over the full source before any selection
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/transcribe.py:159-171 (supadata word times synthesized proportionally within caption chunks), transcribe.py:37-47 and :211-214 (true word timestamps on Whisper paths, error if absent); backend/config.py:26 (TRANSCRIBER default 'supadata'), config.py:254 (PUNCTUATION_ENABLED default on); backend/pipeline/punctuation/service.py:238-269 (build_sentences gated seam, runs before select/understand), service.py:224-235 (_needs_punctuation always true for supadata); backend/pipeline/punctuation/reconstructor.py:1-45 (word/timestamp-preserving sentence rebuild); backend/pipeline/sentences.py:139-148 (guarded-period fragment merge); backend/orchestrator.py:103-104 (sentences built before any selection/structuring)
requirements.notes: Punctuation restoration and fragmented-caption merging over the FULL source before selection are fully implemented and default-on. But on the default TRANSCRIBER=supadata path the 'word-level timestamps' are synthesized (linear interpolation inside 180-char caption chunks, config.py:34) — approximate, not ASR word timing. True full-source word timestamps require TRANSCRIBER=groq/faster_whisper; the default path only gets real word-level timing in localized Whisper windows during boundary refinement (backend/pipeline/boundary.py:70-95).

requirements.requirement: Speaker diarization (pyannote) feeding speaker identity into units/segmentation
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/diarize.py:25-56 (pyannote/speaker-diarization-3.1 pipeline, available() requires HF_TOKEN + pyannote.audio import); backend/config.py:245-246 (DIARIZATION_ENABLED default '0' = OFF), config.py:135 (DEFAULTS['diarization']: False); backend/pipeline/understand/perceive.py:116-127 (diarize wired into perception, default off); backend/pipeline/understand/units.py:181,220-226 (_unit_speaker assigns dominant speaker to each Unit); backend/pipeline/understand/dependencies.py:66-76 (speaker sharpens 'answers' and 'continues' edges); backend/adapters/interview.py:32-35 (speaker-aware labeling hints); backend/pipeline/punctuation/chunker.py:56-93 (speaker-change-aware chunk cuts)
requirements.notes: Complete code path (audio -> SpeakerTurn -> Unit.speaker -> interview/continues edges) exists, but it is default-OFF (DIARIZATION=0, DEFAULTS diarization=False) and pyannote.audio is NOT installed in .venv (import fails), so it cannot run today; cached perception.json files show diarization=[]. Speaker identity does not feed TreeSeg topic segmentation itself, only units and edges (plus punctuation chunking).

requirements.requirement: Keyframe / scene-change detection feeding the structure
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/scenes.py:25-41 (ffmpeg select='gt(scene,THRESHOLD)' scene cuts), :44-49 (uniform grid), :85-137 (detect_and_extract: grid ∪ cuts, dHash de-dup, contiguous Scene spans); backend/config.py:236-240 (SCENE_THRESHOLD, KEYFRAME_GRID_S=15s, KEYFRAME_MAX), config.py:164 (MULTIMODAL default '1' = on); backend/pipeline/understand/perceive.py:87-93 (scenes stage in perception); backend/orchestrator.py:160-177 (perceive runs before build_structure in full profile); work/dHjWVlfNraM/perception.json contains 70 scenes
requirements.notes: Default-on (MULTIMODAL=1, full profile). Scenes feed the structure via keyframe-captioned VisualEvents and Unit.visual_dependencies; confirmed working in cached runs. Caveat: scene boundaries do not influence topic segmentation boundaries (config.py:183 notes embedding/scene segmentation signals as a future upgrade) — they feed the visual layer, not the content map cuts.

requirements.requirement: OCR of on-screen text/equations as visual_events with timestamps
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/vision.py:36-44 (Gemini vision prompt: transcribe on-screen text/equations/code VERBATIM per keyframe), :96-100 (VisualEvent with start/end timestamps, kind incl. equation/slide/code/chart); backend/config.py:242 (VISION_ENGINE default gemini_keyframes), config.py:241 (OCR_ENGINE default 'none'); backend/pipeline/understand/ocr.py:1-95 (optional easyocr/pytesseract merge, default OFF); backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:44-51 (VisualEvent model); work/dHjWVlfNraM/perception.json: 69 visual_events with text like 'Kinematics', kind=slide, timestamps
requirements.notes: On-screen text/equation reading is done by the default-on Gemini-vision keyframe pass (semantic OCR, verbatim text into VisualEvent.text with timestamps), verified in cached output. Classical glyph OCR (easyocr/pytesseract) exists but is default-off (OCR_ENGINE=none) and only merges into vision events. Requirement is functionally satisfied by the default path.

requirements.requirement: Visual events synchronized to transcript with confidence; units carry visual_dependencies
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/vision.py:73-75 (nearby transcript sent with each keyframe), :96-100 (confidence per event); backend/pipeline/understand/units.py:89-106 (_link_visual: temporal overlap ±5s + kind + fuzzy text similarity, threshold 0.4), :195-210 (_build_visual_deps -> VisualDependency with visual_event_id/keyframe_time), :213-217 (_source_confidence: unconfirmed visual dep lowers unit confidence to 0.85); backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:31-51 (VisualDependency, SourceConfidence, VisualEvent.confidence); work/dHjWVlfNraM/structure.json: 51 visual_dependencies, all 51 linked to visual_event_id
requirements.notes: Fully implemented and confirmed in cached structures: events carry confidence, are time-aligned to the transcript, and units declare visual_dependencies resolved to concrete events. Unit-level confidence is a scalar source_confidence (the SourceConfidence transcript/visual/overall model exists but the scalar is what is used, models.py:88).

requirements.requirement: Hierarchical content map (chapter -> topic -> subtopic), deterministic segmentation
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/treeseg.py:1-7 ('No LLM, no randomness — same input, same output'), :52-66 (Ward-style divisive bisection), :104 (chapter_cut from earliest splits); backend/config.py:198 (CONTENT_MAP_ENGINE default 'treeseg'); backend/pipeline/understand/content_map.py:259-286 (_assemble_treeseg builds video/chapter/topic nodes ONLY — no subtopics), :100-120,:173-182 (subtopic splitting exists only in the legacy LLM engine); backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:92-123 (ContentNode levels video|chapter|topic|subtopic); work/dHjWVlfNraM/structure.json content_map levels: {video:1, chapter:3, topic:16} — no subtopic nodes
requirements.notes: Deterministic embedding-based segmentation (TreeSeg + pause/discourse priors) is the default and real; hierarchy is chapter->topic. The subtopic level is only produced by the non-default legacy 'llm' engine (_split_subtopics); the default treeseg path emits a 2-level map, confirmed in cached structures. Labels come from a cheap LLM pass but cannot move boundaries (content_map.py:226-254).

requirements.requirement: Atomic discourse units with role/summary/claims/concepts/equations/references(resolves_to->source_unit)/visual_dependencies/confidence
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:70-89 (Unit: role, role_domain, summary, claims, concepts_introduced, concepts_required, equations, references, visual_dependencies, speaker, source_confidence), models.py:25-28 (Reference.text/resolves_to/source_unit); backend/pipeline/understand/units.py:53-71 (single LLM pass extracts all fields per topic), :172-183 (Unit construction); backend/pipeline/understand/dependencies.py:52-56 (ref.source_unit filled during graph build)
requirements.notes: All designed fields present and populated in one grounded per-topic pass; reference resolution to source_unit happens in the dependency stage as documented. Confidence is the scalar source_confidence (1.0 transcript-only, 0.85 if a declared visual dep is unconfirmed).

requirements.requirement: Universal role ontology (~20 roles exactly as listed)
requirements.evidence: backend/roles.py:12-32 (UniversalRole enum with exactly the 20 listed roles: setup, definition, claim, explanation, intuition, procedure, demonstration, example_setup, worked_step, result, practice_prompt, solution, evidence, misconception, correction, exception, summary, transition, administrative, irrelevant), roles.py:42-47 (NON_ANCHOR set), roles.py:50-57 (coerce_role fallback)
requirements.notes: Exact 1:1 match with the design's 20-role list; closed enum, domain roles layered separately by adapters without touching it.

requirements.requirement: Domain adapters extending universal roles with domain completeness structures
requirements.evidence: backend/adapters/base.py:140-204 (BaseAdapter: _domain_role_specs + _contracts merged over universal), base.py:87-132 (GENERIC_CONTRACTS); backend/adapters/lecture.py:19-83 (physics/math role pack: equation_introduction, derivation, calculation... + derivation/result contracts); backend/adapters/coding.py:14-51 (implementation/debugging contracts); backend/adapters/interview.py:19-53 (question/direct_answer/anecdote contracts); backend/adapters/__init__.py:41-53 (11 registered adapters)
requirements.notes: Adapters supply domain roles, ordered completeness contracts, and labeling/concept prompt hints; universal contracts inherited. Lecture is the fully-worked exemplar; smaller adapters (news/recipe/review/story/sports/debate/tutorial) are thinner but real (roles + contracts each).

requirements.requirement: Typed dependency graph (defines, requires, explains, illustrates, continues, answers, contradicts, summarizes, visually_depends_on, refers_to)
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:126-138 (Edge with typed relation, weight, rationale, derivation); backend/pipeline/understand/dependencies.py:21-22 (_RELATIONS = exactly the 10 designed types), :25-77 (rule edges: requires/refers_to/answers/continues), :80-104 (visually_depends_on edges), :112-146 (bridge-concept requires edges, default on config.py:191), :170-182 (LLM pass adds explains/illustrates/contradicts/summarizes/answers), :185-205 (build + dedupe)
requirements.notes: All 10 relation types are in the accepted vocabulary. Rule edges cover requires/refers_to/answers/continues/visually_depends_on; explains/illustrates/contradicts/summarizes come only from the best-effort LLM refinement pass; 'defines' is accepted but no generator emits it in practice (not in rules, not requested in the LLM prompt) — a minor gap.

requirements.requirement: Context closure: anchor expands to minimal units for standalone comprehension (graph-driven, bounded)
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/assemble/closure.py:91-144 (compute_closure: contract-required elements inlined span-bounded; graph needs via answers/refers_to/requires/visually_depends_on walked weight/nearness-first within budget; far prereqs become referential + truncated flag); closure.py:21-25 (ClosureBudget: max_extra_units=6, max_gap_s=25, max_span_s=300 from config.py:211-213); backend/pipeline/assemble/candidates.py:93-120 (build_candidate wraps closure into a sentence-anchored candidate)
requirements.notes: Faithful to the design including the near/far split (far prerequisites are referenced via the context card / watch-first hint instead of being inlined, keeping clips minimal).

requirements.requirement: Per-clip-type completeness contracts (definition, worked-example, practice, solution) enforced at assembly
requirements.evidence: backend/adapters/base.py:87-132 (contracts for definition, claim, result/worked-example, solution, practice_prompt — practice deliberately excludes the solution element, base.py:111 — procedure, demonstration, explanation, correction); backend/pipeline/assemble/contracts.py:21-32 (deterministic check_contract); backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:344-350 (contract precheck each repair iteration), :280-320 (_fill_contract pulls missing required elements before judging); backend/pipeline/assemble/closure.py:59-88 (contract-required elements force-inlined at closure); backend/adapters/base.py:194-204 (required_verdict_fields ties contracts to judge gating)
requirements.notes: Contracts are enforced three ways at assembly: closure inlines required elements, a deterministic precheck+fill runs before every judge call, and problem-shaped roles must pass problem/reasoning/result judge booleans. Also independently measured by context_complete_rate / worked_example_completeness in eval.

requirements.requirement: Practice<->solution explicit linkage (answered_by) and pairing behavior
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/dependencies.py:56-65 (solution -> 'answers' edge to the nearest prior practice_prompt/example_setup, both via reference resolution and a role-based fallback), :66-72 (interview direct_answer -> question, cross-speaker); backend/adapters/base.py:103-107 (solution contract REQUIRES the prompt 'before'), :108-112 (practice_prompt contract deliberately has no solution element — practice clip stops before the answer); backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:204-208,216-219 (repair pulls problem statement before / result after); backend/pipeline/assemble/closure.py:38-40 ('answers' is the highest-weight closure relation)
requirements.notes: Implemented as an 'answers' edge (solution answers prompt) rather than the name 'answered_by' — semantically the designed linkage. Pairing behavior is enforced: a solution clip must contain/pull its prompt; a practice clip excludes the answer.

requirements.requirement: Three context levels: in-clip context, generated grounded context card, prerequisite-aware sequencing exposed to the user
requirements.evidence: Level 1: backend/pipeline/assemble/closure.py:91-144 (in-clip inlining). Level 2: backend/pipeline/assemble/context_card.py:43-73 (source-only card) and assemble/__init__.py:176-184. Level 3: backend/pipeline/assemble/sequence.py:9-47 (chronological order + prerequisite_clips watch-first hints); backend/orchestrator.py:62-66 (context_card, sequence_index, prerequisite_clips in the clip payload); frontend/src/types.ts:14-16 (fields typed) but frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx renders only facet/time/reason (grep for context_card/prerequisite in components: no hits)
requirements.notes: All three levels exist in the pipeline and are exposed in the API payload. The gap is end-user surfacing: the shipped React UI types the fields but never renders the context card or the watch-first pointers; only the chronological ordering is implicitly visible.

requirements.requirement: Clip-only judge: separate model, sees only the clip, structured verdict with the designed fields
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:33-54 (JudgeVerdict: topic_identifiable, purpose_identifiable, all_references_resolved, prerequisites_satisfied, visuals_sufficient, problem/reasoning/result_complete, source_grounded, failure_reasons with typed kinds, CoT reasoning, score_10), :57-89 (JUDGE_SYSTEM: sees ONLY the clip transcript + on-screen text + optional card), :96-133 (judge_clip routes to a different model; falls back to authoring model on error, honest error verdict never a free pass); backend/config.py:232 (JUDGE_MODEL default gemini-2.5-flash-lite ≠ authoring gemini-2.5-flash config.py:52 -> cross-model active by default), config.py:216-226 (JUDGE_ENABLED default on; cross-PROVIDER judging opt-in, default 'same')
requirements.notes: Verdict schema matches the design field-for-field. Separate-model mitigation is on by default via a different Gemini model (flash-lite); cross-provider is opt-in (Groq key currently invalid per project memory). The judge never sees actual frames — only textual visual_summary (see R18) — matching the 'ideally frames/OCR' clause only partially.

requirements.requirement: Bounded repair loop (~3 attempts) then REJECT; rejections recorded
requirements.evidence: backend/config.py:217 (JUDGE_MAX_REPAIR = 3); backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:344-375 (loop of JUDGE_MAX_REPAIR+1 judge attempts with targeted expand_candidate between them; span cap prevents overgrowth), :163-277 (expansion targeted by verdict booleans + failure kinds, bounded by closure_max_span_s), :382-389 (Rejection stage='repair' recorded on exhaustion); backend/pipeline/assemble/integrity.py:14-26 (Rejection ledger: repair|snap|dedupe|post_merge_judge|quality_floor|max_clips); assemble/__init__.py:84-87,205-207 (rejections collected and surfaced)
requirements.notes: Exactly the designed bounded-expand-then-reject shape with a full 6-stage rejection ledger. Two deliberate softenings: a judge outage ships the clip flagged 'unjudged' instead of rejecting (validate.py:362-363), and a best-partial that passes the hard core (topic+purpose+grounded+refs) ships below the score threshold (validate.py:377-381).

requirements.requirement: Boundary refinement AFTER semantic assembly: word/sentence snapping, pauses, speaker turns, visual transitions, pre/post-roll
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:107-109 (snap runs after judge/repair); backend/pipeline/assemble/boundary_adapt.py:35-66 (metadata-preserving sentence snap + dedupe); backend/pipeline/refine.py:45-120 (_snap_one: start->sentence start, end->period, min/max duration, tail pad); backend/pipeline/boundary.py:96-127 (_pick_start 'begin at a thought' / _pick_end 'complete the thought' via targeted word-level Whisper), orchestrator.py:212-215 (Whisper pass after assembly, PRECISE_BOUNDARIES default on but only for source=='supadata'); config.py:121 (tail_pad_s=0.05); grep for pause/speaker/scene in boundary.py+boundary_adapt.py: no hits
requirements.notes: Word- and sentence-level snapping after semantic assembly is real (sentence snap always; word-level Whisper snap default-on for the supadata path), with start=idea-start / end=period semantics. Missing vs design: boundary refinement uses no pause, speaker-turn, or visual-transition signals, and pre-roll is absent (only a 0.05s post tail-pad). Pauses/speaker changes are used upstream (punctuation, treeseg priors), not at clip boundaries.

requirements.requirement: Judge/eval sees visual context (keyframes or OCR) where the clip depends on visuals
requirements.evidence: In-pipeline: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:136-139 (validate_candidate passes visual_summary_fn(start,end)) and assemble/__init__.py:82,124-126 (structure.visual_summary into judge); backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:159-167 (visual_summary = on-screen text/descriptions overlapping the clip); validate.py:107 (ON-SCREEN TEXT block in judge prompt). Eval: backend/eval/metrics.py:39-40 (comprehension passes visual_summary=""), metrics.py:217 (judge_failures reads s['visual_summary'] which is never populated)
requirements.notes: The production judge/repair loop does see OCR-style visual context (verbatim on-screen text from the vision pass) scoped to the clip span. But the eval comprehension scorer explicitly passes an empty visual_summary, so eval judging is transcript+card only; and neither path ever sends actual keyframe images to the judge (text-only). visuals_sufficient is judged from the transcript's reliance on unshown visuals (validate.py:78-79).

requirements.requirement: Context card generated ONLY from verified source units, grounded to units/timestamps, validated as part of what ships
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/assemble/context_card.py:26-31 (prompt: use ONLY provided unit summaries, cite source_unit_id), :43-52 (allowed set = anchor + referential units only), :58-62 (each sentence must cite an allowed unit AND pass fuzzy entailment _grounded >= 55 else dropped), :64-69 (extractive verbatim fallback, grounded by construction), :70-73 (hard word cap); assemble/__init__.py:30-34,176-184 (missing-card-when-needed penalized as a warning); backend/eval/metrics.py:82-101 (grounding_precision independently re-verifies shipped cards per sentence)
requirements.notes: Generation is source-locked with per-sentence citation+entailment gating and an omit-rather-than-hallucinate policy; eval re-verifies grounding of what ships. Minor deviations: the shipped card is plain text (unit-id citations are validated then dropped, no timestamps in the card), and the in-pipeline judge runs BEFORE cards exist (cards are step 6), so card quality is validated by the grounding gate + eval judge rather than by the repair-loop judge.

requirements.requirement: Prerequisite-aware clip sequencing surfaced to the end user (API + frontend)
requirements.evidence: backend/pipeline/assemble/sequence.py:9-47 (chronological sequence_index + prerequisite_clips computed from concepts_required vs earlier clips' concepts_introduced); backend/orchestrator.py:62-66 (sequence_index, prerequisite_clips, context_card in every clip dict); frontend/src/types.ts:14-16 (Clip type declares the fields); frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx:34-48 (renders only embed, facet, time range, reason — no prerequisite/watch-first/card UI; component grep finds zero usages)
requirements.notes: API payloads fully carry the watch-first data (prerequisite_clips as sequence indices) and clips ship in prerequisite-respecting chronological order. The frontend types the fields but no component renders them, so the end user never actually sees watch-first / assumes / comes-next hints. Note it is hints-only: sequencing never reorders (chronological by design, sequence.py:3-5).

requirements.requirement: Eval metrics per design: role accuracy, anchor recall, context-complete, unresolved-reference, prerequisite-gap, worked-example completeness, visual completeness, grounding precision, boundary error, comprehension, sequence coherence
requirements.evidence: backend/eval/metrics.py — all 11 exist: comprehension :31 (label-free, judge), unresolved_reference_rate :61 (label-free), grounding_precision :82 (label-free), context_complete_rate :122 (label-free), prerequisite_gap_rate :129 (label-free), worked_example_completeness :148 (label-free), visual_completeness :178 (label-free), sequence_coherence :191 (label-free), role_accuracy :225 (gold), anchor_recall :238 (gold), boundary_error :248 (gold); backend/eval/run_eval.py:184-217 (all wired into _measure, gold ones gated on golden file), run_eval.py:8-25 (--runs/--freeze/--freeze-specs variance harness); backend/eval/golden.py:22-29 + eval/golden/ directory is EMPTY (only . and ..); backend/eval/make_golden.py:33-70 (skeleton generator exists)
requirements.notes: Every designed metric is implemented. Computable today: the 8 label-free ones (comprehension, unresolved-reference, context-complete, prerequisite-gap, worked-example, visual-completeness (NaN unless perception ran — 2 cached videos have it), grounding-precision, sequence-coherence) over ~15 cached work/ transcripts. NOT computable today: the 3 golden-gated ones (role_accuracy, anchor_recall, boundary_error) — eval/golden/ contains zero label files; only the skeleton-dump tool exists. Marked partial solely for the missing golden data.

requirements.requirement: Adapter-based universality: identical pipeline stages, only the adapter changes; coverage of lecture/tutorial/coding/interview/debate/news/recipe/review/story/sports
requirements.evidence: backend/orchestrator.py:180-184 (select_adapter then the same build_structure/assemble_clips for every genre); backend/pipeline/understand/build.py:25-53 and backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:45 (adapter is a parameter to the fixed stage sequence); backend/adapters/__init__.py:41-53 (registered: generic, lecture, interview, coding, tutorial, debate, recipe, review, story, sports, news — all 10 requested domains + generic fallback); backend/adapters/detect.py:22-46 (content-type detection -> domain routing, generic fallback), config.py:131 (domain_override per job)
requirements.notes: Stage sequence (content map -> units -> deps -> anchors -> closure -> judge -> snap -> cards -> sequence) is genre-independent; adapters only supply role packs, contracts, and prompt hints. All 10 designed domains have adapters; depth varies (lecture is rich, news/sports/story are ~40-line minimal packs).

overall_verdict: The implementation is a remarkably faithful rendering of the structure-first design: the whole spine — deterministic TreeSeg content map, 20-role universal ontology, unit extraction with concepts/references/equations, typed dependency graph, bounded graph-driven context closure, per-role completeness contracts, clip-only cross-model judge with a 3-attempt repair-then-reject loop and 6-stage rejection ledger, grounded context cards, post-assembly boundary snapping, and all 11 eval metrics — exists in working, default-enabled code, with multimodal perception (scenes + Gemini-vision OCR + linked visual_dependencies) confirmed in cached runs. The biggest daylight is at the edges of perception and delivery: diarization is fully coded but default-off and pyannote is not even installed; the default Supadata path fakes word-level timestamps by interpolation; the default treeseg map has no subtopic level; the judge (and especially the eval judge, which gets an empty visual summary) never sees frames — only on-screen text; boundary refinement ignores pauses/speaker-turns/visual transitions; and the frontend never renders the context cards or watch-first hints the API already ships. Eval-wise, the label-free harness is genuinely trustworthy (freeze/runs/variance), but the three golden-gated metrics (role accuracy, anchor recall, boundary error) are uncomputable because eval/golden/ is empty.

verified_non_met.requirement: Word-level timestamps + punctuation restoration + fragmented-caption merging over the full source before any selection
verified_non_met.evidence: backend/pipeline/transcribe.py:159-171 (supadata word times synthesized proportionally within caption chunks), transcribe.py:37-47 and :211-214 (true word timestamps on Whisper paths, error if absent); backend/config.py:26 (TRANSCRIBER default 'supadata'), config.py:254 (PUNCTUATION_ENABLED default on); backend/pipeline/punctuation/service.py:238-269 (build_sentences gated seam, runs before select/understand), service.py:224-235 (_needs_punctuation always true for supadata); backend/pipeline/punctuation/reconstructor.py:1-45 (word/timestamp-preserving sentence rebuild); backend/pipeline/sentences.py:139-148 (guarded-period fragment merge); backend/orchestrator.py:103-104 (sentences built before any selection/structuring)
verified_non_met.notes: Punctuation restoration and fragmented-caption merging over the FULL source before selection are fully implemented and default-on. But on the default TRANSCRIBER=supadata path the 'word-level timestamps' are synthesized (linear interpolation inside 180-char caption chunks, config.py:34) — approximate, not ASR word timing. True full-source word timestamps require TRANSCRIBER=groq/faster_whisper; the default path only gets real word-level timing in localized Whisper windows during boundary refinement (backend/pipeline/boundary.py:70-95).

verdict.corrected_status: partial — punctuation restoration (config.py:254 default-on; punctuation/service.py:238-269 runs via orchestrator.py:103-104 before any selection) and fragmented-caption merging (sentences.py:139-148, service.py:264-269) are fully implemented over the full source; but on the default TRANSCRIBER='supadata' (config.py:26) word-level timestamps are synthesized by linear interpolation within 180-char caption chunks (transcribe.py:159-171, config.py:34), not true ASR word timing — real word timestamps require non-default TRANSCRIBER=groq/faster_whisper (transcribe.py:37-39, groq_client.py:140-141) or appear only in localized post-selection Whisper windows during boundary refinement (boundary.py:70-95, config.py:62)
verdict.reasoning: Every auditor citation verified and no refuting mechanism found. (1) Default transcriber is supadata: backend/config.py:26; orchestrator routes there at backend/orchestrator.py:84-91. (2) Supadata word times are synthetic: backend/pipeline/transcribe.py:139 (docstring 'Synthesizes per-word times (proportional within each chunk)') and :159-171 (start = ch['start'] + dur*j/n over whitespace tokens of 180-char chunks, config.py:34). (3) True ASR word timestamps only on Whisper paths: transcribe.py:37-39 (faster_whisper word_timestamps=True), backend/groq_client.py:140-141 (timestamp_granularities=['word','segment']), transcribe.py:211-214 (hard error if words absent). (4) Punctuation restoration default-on and pre-selection: config.py:254 (PUNCTUATION default '1'); punctuation/service.py:225-227 (_needs_punctuation always True for supadata source); service.py:238-269 (build_sentences gated seam); orchestrator.py:103-104 (build_sentences runs before the profile switch and _run_full/_run_fast at orchestrator.py:108-120); reconstructor.py:1-7 guarantees word/timestamp preservation. (5) Fragment merging: sentences.py:139-148 (guarded-period merge in segment_sentences) plus sentences_from_chunks fallback at service.py:264-269. (6) On the default path, real word timing exists only in localized windows after selection: boundary.py:70-95 (_whisper_window, word_timestamps=True at :81), invoked post-selection via refine_clip_boundaries (boundary.py:150; orchestrator.py:215, :250), gated by PRECISE_BOUNDARIES default-on (config.py:62). Refutation attempts: grepped all TRANSCRIBER/transcribe_supadata/word_timestamps/interpolate sites in backend/ — no full-source Whisper pass or word-time upgrade mechanism exists on the supadata path (only other hit, sentences.py:250 _interpolate, is also proportional). Therefore the claimed 'partial' stands: 2 of 3 sub-requirements fully met, word-level timestamps only approximately met under the default configuration.

verified_non_met.requirement: Speaker diarization (pyannote) feeding speaker identity into units/segmentation

NOT OPERATIONAL / GAPS (supports 'partial' not 'met'):

verified_non_met.requirement: Hierarchical content map (chapter -> topic -> subtopic), deterministic segmentation
verified_non_met.evidence: backend/pipeline/understand/treeseg.py:1-7 ('No LLM, no randomness — same input, same output'), :52-66 (Ward-style divisive bisection), :104 (chapter_cut from earliest splits); backend/config.py:198 (CONTENT_MAP_ENGINE default 'treeseg'); backend/pipeline/understand/content_map.py:259-286 (_assemble_treeseg builds video/chapter/topic nodes ONLY — no subtopics), :100-120,:173-182 (subtopic splitting exists only in the legacy LLM engine); backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:92-123 (ContentNode levels video|chapter|topic|subtopic); work/dHjWVlfNraM/structure.json content_map levels: {video:1, chapter:3, topic:16} — no subtopic nodes
verified_non_met.notes: Deterministic embedding-based segmentation (TreeSeg + pause/discourse priors) is the default and real; hierarchy is chapter->topic. The subtopic level is only produced by the non-default legacy 'llm' engine (_split_subtopics); the default treeseg path emits a 2-level map, confirmed in cached structures. Labels come from a cheap LLM pass but cannot move boundaries (content_map.py:226-254).

verdict.corrected_status: partial — deterministic segmentation is fully met by the default TreeSeg engine (backend/pipeline/understand/treeseg.py:1-7,52-101; config.py:198 default "treeseg"), but the default path builds only a 2-level chapter→topic map (_assemble_treeseg, content_map.py:259-286); subtopic nodes are produced only by the non-default legacy "llm" engine (content_map.py:100,173-182), and treeseg's own test asserts no subtopics (tests/test_content_map_treeseg.py:50)
verdict.reasoning: Attempted refutation failed on all angles. (1) Default/config: config.py:198 defaults CONTENT_MAP_ENGINE to "treeseg"; no override in .env; per-job settings default None → inherit (config.py:129); dispatcher content_map.py:321-324 confirms routing. (2) Hierarchy: _assemble_treeseg (content_map.py:259-286) creates only video/chapter/topic nodes; grep for level="subtopic" across backend hits only the legacy LLM path (content_map.py:173-182 via _split_subtopics at :100); no adapter/eval/orchestrator/frontend code creates subtopics; test_content_map_treeseg.py:50 explicitly asserts treeseg emits no subtopic nodes. (3) Runtime confirmation: all 5 work/*/structure.json content maps have engine=treeseg and zero subtopic nodes (e.g. work/dHjWVlfNraM: video:1/chapter:3/topic:16). (4) Determinism: treeseg.py:1-7 (no LLM, no randomness), :79 strict tie-break to earliest index, :37-49 deterministic priors, :104-121 chapter cut from same tree; LLM labeling (content_map.py:226-254) only assigns titles/keywords to fixed segment spans and cannot move boundaries. The requirement is half-met (deterministic segmentation yes; chapter→topic→subtopic 3-level hierarchy no on the default path), so the auditor's "partial" rating stands.

Toward 'met': searched frontend/src for context_card|prerequisite|"watch first" — only hit is the type declarations at frontend/src/types.ts:14-16; ClipCard.tsx renders facet (line 43), time/duration (line 45), reason (line 48) only; ResultsGrid.tsx:7 maps clips in received (chronological) order with no prerequisite UI; backend/main.py has no HTML/template rendering path, so no alternate UI surfaces the fields. Toward 'missing': all three levels exist and are unconditional — Level 1 in-clip inlining in compute_closure at backend/pipeline/assemble/closure.py:91-144 (contract-required units inlined span-bounded, graph prerequisites inlined under budget, overflow to referential); Level 2 grounded card at backend/pipeline/assemble/context_card.py:43-90 (LLM card restricted to anchor+referential unit summaries, per-sentence fuzzy-entailment gate at line 34-39 with partial_ratio>=55, extractive verbatim fallback lines 76-80, 40-word cap via config.py:233), invoked for every kept spec at backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:175-184 with no disabling flag; Level 3 at backend/pipeline/assemble/sequence.py:9-47 (chronological sort + sequence_index + earliest-introducer prerequisite_clips hints), called at assemble/__init__.py:187. The orchestrator puts context_card, sequence_index, prerequisite_clips into every clip dict at backend/orchestrator.py:64-66, so the data reaches the client. Hence the auditor's 'partial' (pipeline+API complete, end-user surfacing missing for card and watch-first hints) is exactly right.

verified_non_met.requirement: Boundary refinement AFTER semantic assembly: word/sentence snapping, pauses, speaker turns, visual transitions, pre/post-roll
verified_non_met.evidence: backend/pipeline/assemble/__init__.py:107-109 (snap runs after judge/repair); backend/pipeline/assemble/boundary_adapt.py:35-66 (metadata-preserving sentence snap + dedupe); backend/pipeline/refine.py:45-120 (_snap_one: start->sentence start, end->period, min/max duration, tail pad); backend/pipeline/boundary.py:96-127 (_pick_start 'begin at a thought' / _pick_end 'complete the thought' via targeted word-level Whisper), orchestrator.py:212-215 (Whisper pass after assembly, PRECISE_BOUNDARIES default on but only for source=='supadata'); config.py:121 (tail_pad_s=0.05); grep for pause/speaker/scene in boundary.py+boundary_adapt.py: no hits

Pauses/speaker changes are used upstream (punctuation, treeseg priors), not at clip boundaries.

verdict.corrected_status: partial — sentence snap always runs post-assembly (assemble/__init__.py:107-110 → boundary_adapt.py:35-66 → refine.py:45-120) and word-level Whisper snap is default-on (config.py:62) but only for source=='supadata' (orchestrator.py:212, 247); no pause, speaker-turn, or visual-transition signals at boundary time (zero grep hits in boundary.py/refine.py/boundary_adapt.py/cut.py — those signals live upstream in punctuation/prompt.py:57-79, chunker.py:7-32, understand/scenes.py) and no pre-roll (only 0.05s tail pad, config.py:121; cut.py:72-75 cuts exactly at start)
verdict.reasoning: Verified each auditor citation and adversarially searched for missed mechanisms. (1) Post-assembly snapping confirmed: assemble/__init__.py:107-110 calls snap_candidates after judge/repair; boundary_adapt.py:35-66 wraps refine._snap_one (refine.py:45-120: start→sentence start, end→period, min/max duration enforcement, tail pad); targeted word-level Whisper refine at boundary.py:150-185 with _pick_start/_pick_end (boundary.py:98-127), invoked at orchestrator.py:212-215 and :247-250, gated by PRECISE_BOUNDARIES default-on (config.py:62) AND source=='supadata'. (2) Refutation attempts failed: grep for pause|gap|silence|speaker|scene across boundary.py, refine.py, assemble/boundary_adapt.py, cut.py — zero hits; pause/speaker signals exist only upstream in punctuation restoration (punctuation/prompt.py:57-79 pauseBeforeMs/speaker fields, punctuation/chunker.py:7-32 pause-gap seams) and scene detection only in the understand layer (understand/scenes.py, understand/build.py:4), never threaded into snapping (boundary_adapt.py:54-59 uses units for unit_ids truthfulness only). (3) Pre-roll absent: only post tail_pad_s=0.05 (config.py:121, refine.py:107-108, boundary.py:144,181); cut.py:42-51,72-75 cuts ffmpeg exactly at start; grep for pre_roll|preroll|lead_in|head_pad across backend: no hits. The requirement's 5 signal families are covered as: word/sentence snap = yes, post-roll = minimal, pauses/speaker turns/visual transitions/pre-roll = no. Auditor's 'partial' stands, with the added nuance that the word-level pass silently no-ops for non-supadata (Whisper-sourced) transcripts.

verified_non_met.requirement: Judge/eval sees visual context (keyframes or OCR) where the clip depends on visuals
verified_non_met.evidence: In-pipeline: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:136-139 (validate_candidate passes visual_summary_fn(start,end)) and assemble/__init__.py:82,124-126 (structure.visual_summary into judge); backend/pipeline/understand/models.py:159-167 (visual_summary = on-screen text/descriptions overlapping the clip); validate.py:107 (ON-SCREEN TEXT block in judge prompt). verdict.corrected_status: partial — production judge/repair sees clip-scoped OCR-style on-screen text by default (validate.py:107 via structure.visual_summary, models.py:159-167; vision pass on by default MULTIMODAL=1/VISION_ENGINE=gemini_keyframes, config.py:164,242 — note OCR_ENGINE='none' default is irrelevant since vision.py transcribes on-screen text verbatim); but the eval comprehension scorer hardcodes visual_summary="" (metrics.py:40) and judge_failures reads a spec key never populated (metrics.py:217), so eval judging is transcript+card only; and no path ever sends keyframe images to the judge — judge_clip uses text-only llm_json (validate.py:117, llm.py:59-67), with visuals_sufficient judged blind from transcript reliance (validate.py:78-79)
verdict.reasoning: Every cited line verified and no missed mechanism found. (1) Production judge gets visual text: backend/pipeline/assemble/validate.py:107 puts ON-SCREEN TEXT block in the judge prompt; assemble/__init__.py:82 (repair loop) and :124-126 (post-merge re-judge) pass structure.visual_summary; models.py:159-167 builds it from visual_events overlapping [start,end]. This is on by default: config.py:164 MULTIMODAL=1, config.py:242 VISION_ENGINE=gemini_keyframes; vision.py:1-8,36-44 transcribes on-screen text/equations/code verbatim from keyframe images, feeding Structure.visual_events (build.py:38,50-51) — so OCR_ENGINE='none' (config.py:241) does not disable on-screen text. (2) Eval never gets it: metrics.py:39-40 comprehension() hardcodes visual_summary="" (called from run_eval.py:188 even though the structure is resolved at run_eval.py:113-125); metrics.py:216-217 judge_failures reads s.get("visual_summary","") and a repo-wide grep confirms no code writes that key into specs, so it is always empty. (3) No keyframe images ever reach the judge: judge_clip → llm_json is text-only (validate.py:117, llm.py:59-67); image parts (generate_json_mm/image_part) are used only in the perception stage (vision.py:57). validate.py:78-79 explicitly tells the judge it cannot see frames and to infer visuals_sufficient from the transcript. Refutation attempts (config flags, orchestrator.py, main.py, adapters/, eval/run_eval.py, frontend-relevant paths) found nothing that changes this. The 'partial' status therefore stands exactly as the auditor described.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified every element of the auditor's claim and could not refute it.

1. All 11 metrics implemented, at exactly the cited lines in /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/eval/metrics.py: comprehension :31 (clip-only judge, label-free), unresolved_reference_rate :61, grounding_precision :82, context_complete_rate :122, prerequisite_gap_rate :129, worked_example_completeness :148, visual_completeness :178, sequence_coherence :191 (all label-free), and gold-based role_accuracy :225, anchor_recall :238, boundary_error :248 (each returns NaN without gold labels).

2. All wired into the harness: backend/eval/run_eval.py:184-217 (_measure computes the 8 label-free metrics unconditionally at :188-205; gold metrics gated at :207-211 on gold.get("units")/gold.get("anchors")). Gold loading: run_eval.py:235 calls load_golden; backend/eval/golden.py:20-30 reads eval/golden/<video_id>.json. Variance harness per the claim: run_eval.py:8-25 docstring + parse_eval_args :78-110 (--runs/--freeze/--freeze-specs/--rebuild) + aggregate_over_runs :164-180.

3. The blocking gap is real: /Users/vincentfeng/Documents/practice/clips/backend/eval/golden/ is an empty directory (ls shows only . and ..), and a project-wide find for '*golden*' returns only backend/eval/{golden, golden.py, make_golden.py} — no label files or skeletons anywhere else. So role_accuracy, anchor_recall, and boundary_error can never emit a value today. backend/eval/make_golden.py:33-64 is only a skeleton generator whose output a human must correct and rename (make_golden.py:5-6, :62-63).

4. Auditor's computability notes hold: 18 cached work/*/transcript.json (auditor said ~15 — minor undercount, immaterial), and exactly 2 videos have both video.mp4+perception cached, matching the visual_completeness NaN caveat (metrics.py:178-188 returns NaN with no visual deps; run_eval.py:56-69 _maybe_perceive only uses cached media, never network).

Adversarial checks that failed to overturn: no alternate golden path or env override (GOLDEN_DIR is hardcoded at golden.py:20); no other module computes role/anchor/boundary metrics (grep 'golden' across backend hits only eval/ files plus an unrelated punctuation/service.py); no config flag disables the label-free metrics (they run unconditionally in _measure). The only arguable alternative label is 'met' on a strict all-code-exists reading, but the golden set is itself part of the design (metrics.py:5, golden.py:1 cite spec §14; make_golden.py:1 cites spec §4.2), so 3/11 metrics being permanently NaN makes 'partial' the correct severity.
